D1 Optics L2 Understand Reference

Heating a Tungsten Filament

raise a blackbody's temperature and watch its colour climb from red to white

Every hot object emits a continuous spectrum whose shape depends only on temperature — the Planck blackbody law.

Slide T from candle-warm to star-hot. The filament glow updates from spectral integration against the CIE 1931 observer, and the visible-band spectrum redraws beneath.
